Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 421

though that he was destroyed. He then came to, wiped his face, and

said, ‘Allah has spoken the truth and so has His Messenger.” Abu

Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa related this narration. Allah % said: whee Mah WR eee eb cert 8 Re BN SA sell ao os EN al Aa OF Sas, Bi a A A all Sgf o BIG

hes Ge OILS te tae USS

“Whosoever desires the life of the world and its glitter, to them We shall pay in full (the wages of) their deeds therein, and they will have no diminution therein, They are those for whom there is nothing in the Hereafter but Fire, and vain are the deeds they did therein. And of no effect is that which they used to do,’

On The Day Of Resurrection, The First Matter That One Will Be Held Accountable For Is The Prayer: If It Be Right, Then All Of His Deeds Will Be Right; But If It Be Corrupted (Wrong, Not Correct), Then The Rest Of His Deeds Will Be Corrupted

Abu Hurairah # related that the Prophet #8 said, “Verily, the owned slave will be held accountable for his prayer, and if it is lacking, it will be said to him, ‘Why did you make it deficient?” He will say, ‘O my Lord, you made an owner have control over me, and

422 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

he kept me preoccupied away from my prayer.’ He 4 will say, ‘I did indeed see you steal from his wealth for your own self. Should you not have stolen for yourself from your deeds or from his deeds’ In this manner, Allah will establish the proof upon him.” (Altnad) Al-Hasan related that the Messenger of Allah % said, “On the Day of Resurzection, the first matter for which a woman will be asked will be her prayer, then about her husband - how did she act with him?” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) And this hadith is Mursal, yet good.

Abu Hurairah 4 related that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “Deeds will come on the Day of Resurrection; the Prayer will come and say, ‘O my Lord, T am the prayer.’ He 4 will say, ‘You are indeed upon goodness.’ And the Sadagah (charity) will come and say, ‘O my Lord, Lam the Sadagah.’ He 4 will say, ‘Indeed, you are upon goodness.’ And the Siyaam (fasting) will come and say, ‘O my Lord, I am the Siyaam,’ He 4g will say, ‘Indeed, you are upon goodness.’ Then the {other good) deeds will come, and for each one Allah will say, “Indeed, you are upon goodness.’ Then Islam will come and say, ‘O my Lord, Indeed You are As-Salaam (The One Who is free from all defects and deficiencies) and I am indeed Islam.’ Then Allah will say, ‘Indeed you are upon goodness, Today, by you, I will take, and by you, I will give.” (Ahmad) Allah 48 said:

& A 3 254 IE BG, Sl BEY ey Kngeilt

“And whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it will never be accepted of him, and in the Hereafter he will be one of the losers’)

Anas ibn Maalik - related that he heard the Messenger of Allah 2% say, “The oppressive rulers will be brought on the Day of Resurrection -- including those who passed before me and those who will come after me. Then Allah will say, ‘You are the treasurers of My land and the shepherds of My slaves, and with you is my Bughyah (objective, ie. the message was conveyed to them, or perhaps, they had the opportunity and position to not onlv convey the objectives and teachings of Islam, but to apply and enforce them as well).’ He 3 will say to the one who passed away before me,

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 429

‘What incited you to do as you did?’ He will say, ‘Mercy.’ Allah Jalla Jalaaluhu (All-Mighty Allah is in His All-Mightiness) will say, “You are more merciful to My slaves than I?’ Then He #% will say to the one who will come after me, ‘What incited you to do as you did?’ He will say, ‘Ibecame angry for You.’ Allah will say, “You become more angry than I?’ Then Allah will say, ‘Take them, and block them off into a corer from the corners of the Hellfire.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Jaabir 2 said, “When those who migrated to Al-Habasha returned,

. a young girl from them said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, while we were

seated (in Habasha), an old lady from among them passed by us, and she was carrying a jar of water on her head. Then she passed by a youth from them, and he placed one of his hands between her two shoulders. He then pushed (her), and she fell onto her knees and her jar broke. When she got up, she turned to him and said, ‘You shall know, O treacherous one. When Allah places the Kursee (Footstool) and gathers the first ones and the last ones, hands and legs will speak of what they earned. Then you shall know how my matter and your matter stands with Him, tomorrow.’” The Messenger of Allah 2% said, “She has spoken the truth. How do people glorify Allah when rights are not taken from the strong among them and returned to the weak among them.” (Related by Ibn Abee AdDunyaa) And we have already related the hadith of ‘Abdullah ibn Unais 4s, in which the following is mentioned: “Indeed, Allah will call out to His slaves on the Day of Resurrection. He 4 will say, ‘I am the King, lam Ad-Dayyaan, and it is not suitable for anyone from the people of Paradise to enter Paradise whilst anyone from the people of the Hellfire has a wrong with him, nor for anyone from the dwellers of the Hellfire to enter the Hellfire whilst anyone from the people of Paradise has a wrong with him - until I settle it by taking it from him, even if it is a slap.” Ahmad related it, and AlBukhaaree mentioned it Ta’leequn in his sahih.

Abu Hurairah & related that the Prophet 2 said, “Whoever has a wrong with his brother (ie. he did wrong to him), then let him seek to be absolved of it from him, for there (ie. on the Day of Resurrection) there is neither dinar nor dirham (no currency with which to repay others with, except for deeds): his good deeds will be taken, and if he has no good deeds, then he takes from the bad deeds of his brother, which are thrown onto him.” (Bukhaaree and

424 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

In another narration, Abu Hurairah < related that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “Do you know who the bankrupt one is?” 'The Companions 4 said, “He who has neither Ditham nor dinar.” He #8 said, “Rather the bankrupt one is he from my nation who comes on the Day of Resurrection with prayer, fasting, and Zakaat (compulsory charity), yet he comes having cursed this one, wrongfully taken from the wealth of this one, shed the blood of this one, and hit this one. Then this one will take settlement from his good deeds, and this one (will take) from his good deeds, and if his good deeds are finished before he pays what is upon him to pay, their bad deeds are taken and thrown upon him. Then he is thrown into the Hellfire.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Ibn ‘Umar .# related that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “Do not die while upon you is a debt, for there (Le. on the Day of Resurrection), there is neither dinar nor ditham; rather, there are only good deeds — a reward returned for a reward — and your Lord does not do wrong to anyone.” (Related by ibn Abee AdDunyaa)

Retribution Exacted From The Wrongdoers On the Day Of Resurrection

Jaabir 4s related that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “Indeed, a slave will come on the Day of Resurrection, and his good deeds will please him. Then a man will come and say, ‘O my Lord, this one wronged me.’ Then some of his good deeds are taken and placed among the good deeds of the one who asked him. This will continue in the same manner until no good deed remains for him. Then, when someone comes to ask him, his bad deeds (i.e. the bad deeds of the one who is asking) are looked at, and then are placed with the bad deeds of that man (ie. the man who wronged him). His accounts will continue to be settled (in the said manner) until he enters the Hellfire.” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 425

Ash-Shirk Billah (Associating Partners With Allah) Is Not Forgiven, And Wrong Done Among The Slaves (Of Allah) Will Most Certainly Be Settled And Paid Back On The Day Of Resurrection

’ Aishah % related that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “The records with Allah are three: A record that Allah does not mind at all, a record from which Allah will leave nothing out, and a record that Allah will not forgive. As for the record that Allah will not forgive, then that is Shirk (associating partners with Allah). Allah 8€ said:

And as for the record that Allah does not mind at all, it is the
content_id:
Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 421

dia mengira dirinya binasa. Kemudian dia sadar, mengusap wajahnya, dan berkata, 'Allah telah berbicara benar dan begitu pula Rasul-Nya.' Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa meriwayatkan narasi ini. Allah # berfirman: [ayat Arab]

"Barangsiapa menghendaki kehidupan dunia dan perhiasannya, niscaya Kami berikan kepada mereka balasan pekerjaan mereka di dunia dengan sempurna dan mereka di dunia itu tidak akan dirugikan. Itulah orang-orang yang tidak memperoleh di akhirat, kecuali neraka dan lenyaplah di akhirat itu apa yang telah mereka usahakan di dunia dan sia-sialah apa yang telah mereka kerjakan."

Pada Hari Kebangkitan, Hal Pertama yang Seorang Akan Dimintai Pertanggungjawaban Adalah Shalat: Jika Shalatnya Baik, Maka Semua Amalnya Pun Baik; Namun Jika Shalatnya Rusak (Salah, Tidak Benar), Maka Amal-Amal Lainnya Pun Akan Rusak

Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi # bersabda, "Sesungguhnya hamba yang dimiliki akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas shalatnya, dan jika kurang, akan dikatakan kepadanya, 'Mengapa kamu membuatnya kurang?' Dia akan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, Engkau membuat seorang majikan memiliki kendali atasku, dan

422 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

dia membuatku sibuk dari shalatku.' Dia # akan berfirman, 'Aku memang melihatmu mencuri dari hartanya untuk dirimu sendiri. Tidakkah seharusnya kamu mencuri untuk dirimu sendiri dari amalmu atau dari amalnya?' Dengan cara ini, Allah akan menegakkan hujjah atasnya." (Ahmad) Al-Hasan meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Pada Hari Kebangkitan, hal pertama yang akan ditanyakan kepada seorang wanita adalah tentang shalatnya, kemudian tentang suaminya — bagaimana dia bersikap kepadanya?" (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) Dan hadith ini Mursal, namun baik.

Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Amal-amal akan datang pada Hari Kebangkitan; Shalat akan datang dan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, aku adalah shalat.' Dia # akan berfirman, 'Kamu memang dalam kebaikan.' Dan Shadaqah (sedekah) akan datang dan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, aku adalah shadaqah.' Dia # akan berfirman, 'Sesungguhnya kamu dalam kebaikan.' Dan Siyaam (puasa) akan datang dan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, aku adalah siyaam.' Dia # akan berfirman, 'Sesungguhnya kamu dalam kebaikan.' Kemudian amal-amal (baik lainnya) akan datang, dan untuk masing-masing Allah akan berfirman, 'Sesungguhnya kamu dalam kebaikan.' Kemudian Islam akan datang dan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, sesungguhnya Engkau adalah As-Salaam (Yang bebas dari segala cacat dan kekurangan) dan aku sesungguhnya adalah Islam.' Kemudian Allah akan berfirman, 'Sesungguhnya kamu dalam kebaikan. Hari ini, dengan kamulah Aku mengambil, dan dengan kamulah Aku memberi.'" (Ahmad) Allah # berfirman:

"Barangsiapa mencari agama selain agama Islam, maka sekali-kali tidaklah akan diterima (agama itu) daripadanya, dan dia di akhirat termasuk orang-orang yang rugi."

Anas ibn Maalik # meriwayatkan bahwa dia mendengar Rasulullah # bersabda, "Para penguasa yang zalim akan dibawa pada Hari Kebangkitan — termasuk mereka yang mendahului aku dan mereka yang akan datang setelahku. Kemudian Allah akan berfirman, 'Kalian adalah para penjaga tanah-Ku dan para gembala hamba-hamba-Ku, dan bersama kalian ada Bughyah-Ku (tujuan, yakni pesan telah disampaikan kepada mereka, atau mungkin, mereka memiliki kesempatan dan posisi untuk tidak hanya menyampaikan tujuan dan ajaran Islam, tetapi untuk menerapkan dan menegakkannya pula).' Dia # akan berkata kepada yang meninggal sebelumku,

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 429

'Apa yang mendorongmu melakukan apa yang kamu lakukan?' Dia akan berkata, 'Kasih sayang.' Allah Jalla Jalaaluhu (Allah Yang Maha Agung dalam Keagungan-Nya) akan berfirman, 'Apakah kamu lebih penyayang kepada hamba-hamba-Ku daripada Aku?' Kemudian Dia # akan berkata kepada yang akan datang setelahku, 'Apa yang mendorongmu melakukan apa yang kamu lakukan?' Dia akan berkata, 'Aku marah karena-Mu.' Allah akan berfirman, 'Apakah kamu lebih marah dari Aku?' Kemudian Allah akan berfirman, 'Ambil mereka, dan sumbat mereka ke sudut dari sudut-sudut neraka.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Jaabir # berkata, "Ketika orang-orang yang berhijrah ke Al-Habasha kembali,

seorang gadis muda dari mereka berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, ketika kami sedang duduk (di Habasha), seorang wanita tua dari mereka lewat di depan kami, dan dia membawa sebuah kendi air di atas kepalanya. Kemudian dia melewati seorang pemuda dari mereka, dan dia meletakkan salah satu tangannya di antara kedua bahunya. Kemudian dia mendorongnya, dan dia jatuh dengan bertumpu pada lutut-lututnya dan kendinya pecah. Ketika dia berdiri, dia berbalik kepadanya dan berkata, "Kamu akan tahu, wahai pengkhianat! Ketika Allah meletakkan Kursi (Pijakan Kaki) dan mengumpulkan orang-orang pertama dan terakhir, tangan-tangan dan kaki-kaki akan berbicara tentang apa yang mereka kerjakan. Kemudian kamu akan tahu bagaimana keadaanku dan keadaanmu di hadapan-Nya, esok hari."'" Rasulullah # bersabda, "Dia telah berbicara benar. Bagaimana manusia bertasbih kepada Allah sementara hak-hak tidak diambil dari yang kuat di antara mereka dan dikembalikan kepada yang lemah di antara mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) Dan kami telah meriwayatkan hadith 'Abdullah ibn Unais # sebelumnya, yang di dalamnya disebutkan yang berikut: "Sesungguhnya Allah akan berseru kepada hamba-hamba-Nya pada Hari Kebangkitan. Dia # akan berfirman, 'Aku adalah Raja, Aku adalah Ad-Dayyaan, dan tidak pantas bagi siapa pun dari orang-orang surga untuk memasuki surga sementara ada seseorang dari orang-orang neraka yang memiliki kezaliman terhadapnya, dan tidak pula bagi siapa pun dari penghuni neraka untuk memasuki neraka sementara ada seseorang dari orang-orang surga yang memiliki kezaliman terhadapnya — sampai aku menyelesaikannya dengan mengambilnya darinya, meskipun hanya sebuah tamparan.'" Ahmad meriwayatkannya, dan Al-Bukhari menyebutkannya secara Ta'liqan dalam sahih-nya.

Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi # bersabda, "Barangsiapa yang memiliki kezaliman terhadap saudaranya (yakni dia berbuat zalim kepadanya), maka biarlah dia meminta untuk dibebaskan dari kezaliman itu olehnya, karena di sana (yakni pada Hari Kebangkitan) tidak ada dinar maupun dirham (tidak ada mata uang untuk membayar orang lain kecuali amal-amal): amal-amal baiknya akan diambil, dan jika dia tidak memiliki amal baik, maka dia mengambil dari amal-amal buruk saudaranya, yang dilemparkan padanya." (Bukhari dan

424 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Dalam riwayat lain, Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Tahukah kalian siapa orang yang bangkrut itu?" Para sahabat # berkata, "Dia yang tidak memiliki dirham maupun dinar." Beliau # bersabda, "Bahkan orang yang bangkrut dari umatku adalah dia yang datang pada Hari Kebangkitan dengan shalat, puasa, dan zakat (sedekah wajib), namun dia datang setelah mencaci orang ini, mengambil harta orang ini secara tidak sah, menumpahkan darah orang ini, dan memukul orang ini. Kemudian orang ini akan mengambil penyelesaian dari amal-amal baiknya, dan orang ini (akan mengambil) dari amal-amal baiknya, dan jika amal-amal baiknya habis sebelum dia membayar apa yang harus dia bayar, amal-amal buruk mereka diambil dan dilemparkan kepadanya. Kemudian dia dilemparkan ke dalam neraka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Ibn 'Umar # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Janganlah mati sementara ada hutang padamu, karena di sana (yakni pada Hari Kebangkitan) tidak ada dinar maupun dirham; melainkan hanya ada amal-amal baik — pahala dibalas dengan pahala — dan Tuhanmu tidak berbuat zalim kepada siapa pun." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Retribusi yang Diambil dari Para Pelaku Kezaliman pada Hari Kebangkitan

Jaabir # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Sesungguhnya seorang hamba akan datang pada Hari Kebangkitan, dan amal-amal baiknya akan menyenangkannya. Kemudian seorang pria akan datang dan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, orang ini berbuat zalim kepadaku.' Kemudian sebagian dari amal-amal baiknya diambil dan diletakkan di antara amal-amal baik orang yang meminta. Ini akan berlanjut dengan cara yang sama sampai tidak tersisa satu pun amal baik baginya. Kemudian, ketika seseorang datang untuk memintanya, amal-amal buruknya (yakni amal-amal buruk dari orang yang meminta) diperiksa, dan kemudian diletakkan bersama amal-amal buruk pria itu (yakni pria yang berbuat zalim kepadanya). Perhitungannya akan terus diselesaikan (dengan cara yang disebutkan) sampai dia masuk neraka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa)

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 425

Asy-Syirk Billah (Menyekutukan Allah) Tidak Diampuni, dan Kezaliman yang Dilakukan di Antara Para Hamba (Allah) Pasti Akan Diselesaikan dan Dibalas pada Hari Kebangkitan

'Aisyah # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Catatan-catatan pada Allah ada tiga: Sebuah catatan yang Allah tidak peduli sama sekali, sebuah catatan yang Allah tidak akan meninggalkan apa pun dari dalamnya, dan sebuah catatan yang Allah tidak akan mengampuni. Adapun catatan yang Allah tidak akan mengampuni, maka itu adalah Syirik (menyekutukan Allah). Allah # berfirman:

Adapun catatan yang Allah tidak peduli sama sekali, itu adalah
content_ar: الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 421 though that he was destroyed. He then came to, wiped his face, and said, ‘الله has spoken the الحق and لذا has His Messenger.” أبو بكر ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa related this narration. الله % said: whee Mah WR eee eb cert 8 Re BN SA sell ao os EN al Aa OF Sas, Bi a A A all Sgf o BIG hes Ge OILS te tae USS “Whosoever desires the الحياة of the الدنيا and its glitter, to them We shall pay in full (the wages of) their deeds therein, and they will have no diminution therein, They are those for whom there is nothing in الآخرة but Fire, and vain are the deeds they did therein. And of no effect is that which they used to do,’ On The Day Of البعث, The أولاً Matter That One Will Be Held Accountable For Is The الصلاة: إذا It Be الحق, Then All Of His Deeds Will Be الحق; But إذا It Be Corrupted (الخطأ, Not Correct), Then The Rest Of His Deeds Will Be Corrupted أبو هريرة # related that النبي ﷺ #8 said, “Verily, the owned slave will be held accountable for his الصلاة, and إذا it is lacking, it will be said to him, ‘Why did you make it deficient?” He will say, ‘O my Lord, you made an owner have control over me, and 422 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations he kept me preoccupied away from my الصلاة.’ He 4 will say, ‘I did indeed see you steal from his الثروة for your own self. Should you not have stolen for yourself from your deeds or from his deeds’ In this manner, الله will establish the proof upon him.” (Altnad) Al-حسن related that الرسول ﷺ of الله % said, “On the Day of Resurzection, the أولاً matter for which a woman will be asked will be her الصلاة, then about her الزوج - how did she act with him?” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) And this الحديث is Mursal, yet الخير. أبو هريرة 4 related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “Deeds will come on the Day of البعث; the الصلاة will come and say, ‘O my Lord, T am the الصلاة.’ He 4 will say, ‘You are indeed upon goodness.’ And the Sadagah (الصدقة) will come and say, ‘O my Lord, Lam the Sadagah.’ He 4 will say, ‘Indeed, you are upon goodness.’ And the Siyaam (الصيام) will come and say, ‘O my Lord, I am the Siyaam,’ He 4g will say, ‘Indeed, you are upon goodness.’ Then the {other الخير) deeds will come, and for each one الله will say, “Indeed, you are upon goodness.’ Then الإسلام will come and say, ‘O my Lord, Indeed You are As-Salaam (The One Who is free from all defects and deficiencies) and I am indeed الإسلام.’ Then الله will say, ‘Indeed you are upon goodness, Today, by you, I will take, and by you, I will give.” (أحمد) الله 48 said: & A 3 254 IE BG, Sl BEY ey Kngeilt “And whoever seeks a religion other than الإسلام, it will never be accepted of him, and in الآخرة he will be one of the losers’) أنس ibn Maalik - related that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% say, “The oppressive rulers will be brought on the Day of البعث -- بما في ذلك those who passed قبل me and those who will come بعد me. Then الله will say, ‘You are the treasurers of My land and the shepherds of My slaves, and with you is my Bughyah (objective, ie. the message was conveyed to them, or perhaps, they had the opportunity and position to not onlv convey the objectives and teachings of الإسلام, but to apply and enforce them as well).’ He 3 will say to the one who passed away قبل me, الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 429 ‘What incited you to do as you did?’ He will say, ‘الرحمة.’ الله Jalla Jalaaluhu (All-Mighty الله is in His All-Mightiness) will say, “You are more merciful to My slaves than I?’ Then He #% will say to the one who will come بعد me, ‘What incited you to do as you did?’ He will say, ‘Ibecame angry for You.’ الله will say, “You become more angry than I?’ Then الله will say, ‘Take them, and block them off into a corer from the corners of the النار.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) Jaabir 2 said, “عندما those who migrated to Al-Habasha returned, . a young girl from them said, ‘O Messenger of الله, بينما we were seated (in Habasha), an old lady from among them passed by us, and she was carrying a jar of water on her head. Then she passed by a youth from them, and he placed one of his hands between her two shoulders. He then pushed (her), and she fell onto her knees and her jar broke. عندما she got up, she turned to him and said, ‘You shall know, O treacherous one. عندما الله places the Kursee (Footstool) and gathers the أولاً ones and the last ones, hands and legs will speak of what they earned. Then you shall know how my matter and your matter stands with Him, tomorrow.’” الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “She has spoken the الحق. How do people glorify الله عندما الحقوق are not taken from the strong among them and returned to the weak among them.” (Related by Ibn Abee AdDunyaa) And we have already related the الحديث of ‘Abdullah ibn Unais 4s, in which the following is mentioned: “Indeed, الله will call out to His slaves on the Day of البعث. He 4 will say, ‘I am the King, lam Ad-Dayyaan, and it is not suitable for anyone from the people of الجنة to enter الجنة whilst anyone from the people of the النار has a الخطأ with him, nor for anyone from the dwellers of the النار to enter the النار whilst anyone from the people of الجنة has a الخطأ with him - حتى I settle it by taking it from him, even إذا it is a slap.” أحمد related it, and AlBukhaaree mentioned it Ta’leequn in his صحيح. أبو هريرة & related that النبي ﷺ 2 said, “Whoever has a الخطأ with his الأخ (ie. he did الخطأ to him), then let him seek to be absolved of it from him, for there (ie. on the Day of البعث) there is neither dinar nor dirham (no currency with which to repay others with, except for deeds): his الخير deeds will be taken, and إذا he has no الخير deeds, then he takes from the bad deeds of his الأخ, which are thrown onto him.” (Bukhaaree and 424 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations In another narration, أبو هريرة < related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “Do you know who the bankrupt one is?” 'The الصحابة 4 said, “He who has neither Ditham nor dinar.” He #8 said, “Rather the bankrupt one is he from my nation who comes on the Day of البعث with الصلاة, الصيام, and Zakaat (compulsory الصدقة), yet he comes having cursed this one, wrongfully taken from the الثروة of this one, shed the blood of this one, and hit this one. Then this one will take settlement from his الخير deeds, and this one (will take) from his الخير deeds, and إذا his الخير deeds are finished قبل he pays what is upon him to pay, their bad deeds are taken and thrown upon him. Then he is thrown into the النار.” (Related by Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) Ibn ‘عمر .# related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “Do not die بينما upon you is a debt, for there (Le. on the Day of البعث), there is neither dinar nor ditham; rather, there are only الخير deeds — a الأجر returned for a الأجر — and your Lord does not do الخطأ to anyone.” (Related by ibn Abee AdDunyaa) Retribution Exacted From The Wrongdoers On the Day Of البعث Jaabir 4s related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “Indeed, a slave will come on the Day of البعث, and his الخير deeds will please him. Then a man will come and say, ‘O my Lord, this one wronged me.’ Then some of his الخير deeds are taken and placed among the الخير deeds of the one who asked him. This will continue in the same manner حتى no الخير deed remains for him. Then, عندما someone comes to ask him, his bad deeds (i.e. the bad deeds of the one who is asking) are looked at, and then are placed with the bad deeds of that man (ie. the man who wronged him). His accounts will continue to be settled (in tقال manner) حتى he enters the النار.” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 425 Ash-الشرك Billah (Associating Partners With الله) Is Not Forgiven, And الخطأ Done Among The Slaves (Of الله) Will Most Certainly Be Settled And Paid Back On The Day Of البعث ’ عائشة % related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “The records with الله are three: A record that الله does not العقل at all, a record from which الله will leave nothing out, and a record that الله will not forgive. أما the record that الله will not forgive, then that is الشرك (associating partners with الله). الله 8€ said: And أما the record that الله does not العقل at all, it is the
